channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Matteo Berrettini gives Italy a 1-0 lead over the Netherlands in the Davis Cup final
item.description: Matteo Berrettini has put Italy ahead 1-0 against the Netherlands in the final of the Davis Cup by beating Botic van de Zandschulp 6-4, 6-2.
item.dc:creator: Howard Fendrich, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Salah double sends Liverpool 8 points clear in Premier League before Amorim's first game with United
item.description: Mohamed Salah sent Liverpool eight points clear at the top of the Premier League by scoring twice to help his team rally to a 3-2 win over last-place Southampton on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: St. Louis Blues fire Drew Bannister and hire Jim Montgomery as coach
item.description: Drew Bannister is out as coach of the St. Louis Blues after less than a full calendar year on the job. Jim Montgomery is back behind the bench of an NHL team five days after he was fired by the Boston Bruins.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SJSU volleyball team with trans player clinches playoff meeting vs. team that forfeited amid controversy
item.description: San Jose State's volleyball team is currently guaranteed to face a team in the upcoming Mountain West Tournament that has already forfeited to it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russell and Hamilton give Mercedes a 1-2 finish in Las Vegas
item.description: George Russell won the Formula 1 race Saturday night in the Las Vegas Grand Prix and his Mercedes teammate Lewis Hamilton was second.
item.dc:creator: Mark Anderson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Max Verstappen wins 4th straight F1 world championship as George Russell wins Las Vegas Grand Prix
item.description: Max Verstappen finished in fifth place at the Las Vegas Grand Prix and it was enough to capture the fourth consecutive F1 world championship of his career.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arnold, Robinson run for more than 100 yards as Oklahoma stuns No. 7 Alabama 24-3
item.description: NORMAN, Okla. (AP) — Xavier Robinson ran for career highs of 107 yards and two touchdowns, and Oklahoma stunned No. 7 Alabama 24-3 on Saturday night to become bowl eligible and deal a severe blow to the Crimson Tide's chances of receiving a College Football Playoff berth.
item.dc:creator: CLIFF BRUNT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Barbra Banda’s goal leads Orlando over Washington 1-0 for NWSL championship. Marta earns title
item.description: Barbra Banda scored in the 37th minute and the Orlando Pride beat the Washington Spirit 1-0 to win their first National Women’s Soccer League championship.
item.dc:creator: Avery Osen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Utah HC busts out of offensive slump with 6-1 victory
item.description: PITTSBURGH (ABC4 Sports) - After getting shut out for the fifth time of the season, the Utah Hockey team scored a regulation-time season-high six goals in a 6-1 rout over the Pittsburgh Penguins Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Barnes leads Aggies to 41-20 win over SDSU
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After a rough start to the season, Utah State is finishing strong. Bryson Barnes accounted for 323 yards and four touchdowns to lead Utah State over San Diego State 41-20 on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Trump Cabinet pick Scott Turner scored only NFL pick 6 in lone bright moment of historically awful season
item.description: Scott Turner, a seventh-round pick out of Illinois, played for the Chargers in 2000 on a team that went 1-15, one of the worst in NFL history.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man City routed 4-0 by Tottenham in fifth-straight defeat as crisis deepens for Pep Guardiola
item.description: It started as an evening of celebration for Manchester City. It ended with the four-time defending Premier League champion conceding four goals and falling to a fifth-straight loss in all competitions as it faces a deepening crisis in a season that is threatening to unravel.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 24 Illinois stuns Rutgers on Bryant's 40-yard TD reception with 4 seconds left
item.description: PISCATAWAY, N.J. (AP) — Trailing by one at the 40-yard line with 14 seconds to play and no timeouts, Illinois coach Bret Bielema sent in a play known as “church” to Luke Altmyer. The quarterback was to hit a receiver who was to go to the ground immediately.
item.dc:creator: TOM CANAVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hidalgo leads No. 6 Notre Dame over JuJu Watkins and third-ranked USC 74-61 in big matchup out West
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hannah Hidalgo scored 24 points and No. 6 Notre Dame defeated JuJu Watkins and third-ranked Southern California 74-61 on Saturday in a marquee matchup on the West Coast. Watkins and the Trojans (4-1) fell behind early and were down 21 points in the fourth quarter.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida knocks No. 9 Ole Miss out of College Football Playoff contention, 24-17 in the Swamp
item.description: GAINESVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Coach Billy Napier and his team did something no one at Florida had done in nearly two decades. It could be the turning point for a once-proud program that clearly slipped in recent years.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida delivers crushing blow to No. 9 Ole Miss' College Football Playoff hopes with upset win
item.description: The Florida Gators delivered a crushing blow to the Ole Miss Rebels' playoff hopes, upsetting the Rebels 24-17 Saturday by sealing the win with two big interceptions late.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Novak Djokovic announces he has hired longtime rival Andy Murray to be next coach
item.description: Novak Djokovic announced on social media on Saturday that longtime rival Andy Murray would be his coach for the offseason and through the Australian Open.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jannik Sinner and Matteo Berrettini lift Italy past Australia and back to the Davis Cup final
item.description: MALAGA, Spain (AP) — Top-ranked Jannik Sinner and Matteo Berrettini won matches Saturday in front of a supportive crowd to lift defending champion Italy past Australia 2-0 and back into the Davis Cup final. Sinner extended his tour-level winning streak to 24 singles sets in a row by beating No.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Andy Murray will coach Novak Djokovic through the Australian Open
item.description: The recently retired Andy Murray is going to team up with longtime rival Novak Djokovic as his coach, they both announced Saturday, with plans to prepare for — and work together through — the Australian Open in January.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Purdy, Bosa out for Green Bay matchup, Allen steps up as QB
item.description: (KRON) – San Francisco 49ers head coach Kyle Shanahan announced in a press conference Friday that quarterback Brock Purdy will be out for Sunday’s game against the Green Bay Packers. Purdy injured his right shoulder in last Sunday's loss to the Seattle Seahawks.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Montrose football aims to earn spot in 4A final four
item.description: The only Western Slope high school football team still alive and still fighting for a title is the 11-0 Montrose Red Hawks, the 2-seed in 4A.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Teen Paves the Way for Female Hockey Players
item.description: Chloe Jezek, a 16-year-old high school hockey player in North County, is paving the way for other girls in the male-dominated sport.
item.dc:creator: Allison Edmonds, Fox 5

item.title: 20 years later: A look back at the Malice at the Palace
item.description: (WXIN/WTTV) — It has now been 20 whole years since the most infamous night in Indiana Pacers franchise history: The Malice at the Palace. One of the worst brawls in U.S. sports history, the incident occurred on Nov.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, NewsNation

item.title: BYU still controls own destiny for Big 12 Championship
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU is in a position it hasn't been in all season long -- trying to bounce back from a loss. The Cougars suffered their first defeat of the season Saturday night, a disappointing 17-13 loss to Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
